title: filtering behavior correction
body:
when filtering containers, if a status= is provided as an input filter, then we should override the all to always be true.

Signed-off-by: Brent Baude <bbaude@redhat.com>

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *baude* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [baude] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

Wondering, if this would not suffer with the same problem as filtering with ID does. Let me explain.
(just note that `status=(created|restarting|running|removing|paused|exited|dead)`)

When `all=true` and `filters.id=<some_id>` then all containers are returned, ignoring the filter. Without `all=true` the filtering works correctly as long as the container is running, when the container is not running, it is not considered (explained [here](https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/5171#issuecomment-585079991)). So with change in this PR wouldn't it always return all containers ignoring the status filter?
